title: P-019: mechanical authorization for session-holder registration (ambient-coordinator rule)
description: Owned files: .harness/bin/session.py AND .harness/README.md ONLY (do NOT touch check_lock.py or harness_common.py - session_holders() semantics unchanged; the gate lives at registration time). (1) register/unregister refuse unless hc.agent_id() (AMBIENT identity, not --by) is in the coordinator set: {'main'} union {names in state.json agents.registry whose role == 'coordinator'}. Read the registry live from state.json, no hardcoded names beyond 'main'. Refusal: exit 1, clear message naming the ambient identity and the rule, and hc.log_event('session_holder_register_refused', agent=<explicit ambient>, name=<requested>, by=<--by value>). (2) Add optional --task flag to register; include task field in the session_holder_registered event (OQ2 attribution fix). (3) Docstring + README Identity section: one honest paragraph - this guards the sanctioned CLI path against ACCIDENTAL protocol violations (a cross-session runner following protocol exports its own CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID and thus gets refused); direct edits to session_holders.json are outside the trust model, same boundary as the fail-open hook. Acceptance probes: (a) ambient main register X -> exit 0 (T-037 replay preserved); (b) CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=fake-cross-engine register fake-cross-engine -> exit 1 + refusal event + absent from list (the T-042 bypass flips to blocked: rerun the full bypass repro - lock held by fake-cross-engine, synthetic check_lock payload me=main -> EXIT 2 stays 2 because registration never happened); (c) unregister same rule; (d) register --task T-053 logs task field; (e) regression: list/expiry behavior unchanged, py_compile clean; clean up all scratch registrations/locks.

notes.note: plan: gate session.py register/unregister on ambient hc.agent_id() in {main} union state.json agents.registry role==coordinator (read live, fail-closed to {main}); log session_holder_register_refused + exit 1 on refusal; add --task to register + OQ2 attribution; update session.py docstring + README Identity section with honest threat-model paragraph; run probes a-e.

DONE. Owned files only: .harness/bin/session.py + .harness/README.md (git diff --stat confirms: session.py +104/-4, README.md +33/-4; no other files touched by me; check_lock.py/harness_common.py untouched per contract).

IMPLEMENTATION: added _coordinator_set() (reads state.json agents.registry live, {'main'} union names with role=='coordinator', try/except falls back to {'main'} alone on any missing/malformed shape -- fail-closed, commented) and _authorize_or_exit(action,name,by) gating on hc.agent_id() (AMBIENT, never --by/name) called at the top of both register() and unregister(). Refusal: hc.log_event('session_holder_register_refused', agent=<ambient>, action=<register|unregister>, name=<requested>, by=<--by or None>) then sys.exit(msg) (prints to stderr, exit 1) naming the ambient identity + coordinator set + the rule. Added --task to register (default None), stamped into the holders.json entry and the session_holder_registered event (OQ2 attribution). Docstring (session.py) + README.md #Identity both got an honest paragraph: guards the sanctioned CLI against ACCIDENTAL protocol violations (a cross-session runner following protocol exports its own CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID and is refused); direct hand-edits of session_holders.json are outside this trust model, same boundary as the fail-open check_lock.py hook.

PROBES (exact commands + observed results; scratch names t053-prefixed, all cleaned up):
(a) T-037 replay: `python3 .harness/bin/session.py register scratch-t053-proba --by substrate-worker-1 --ttl 60 --task T-053` -> stdout "registered: ... task=T-053", EXIT 0. events.jsonl: {"event":"session_holder_registered","agent":"main","holder":"scratch-t053-proba","by":"substrate-worker-1","task":"T-053"}. EXPECTED ambient-main-with-different---by pattern preserved = ACTUAL. Cleaned: `session.py unregister scratch-t053-proba` -> EXIT 0 "unregistered".
(b) Cross-session refusal + full T-042 bypass repro: `CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=fake-cross-engine python3 .harness/bin/session.py register fake-cross-engine` -> stderr "REFUSED: ambient identity 'fake-cross-engine' is not a coordinator (coordinator set = ['fable-5-coordinator', 'main']); session-holder register is a coordinator-only act...", EXIT 1; `session.py list` confirms fake-cross-engine absent; events.jsonl: {"event":"session_holder_register_refused","agent":"fake-cross-engine","action":"register","name":"fake-cross-engine","by":"fake-cross-engine"}. Full bypass repro: `lock.py acquire scratch_probe_t053.tmp --holder fake-cross-engine --task T-053` (EXIT0) then piped synthetic PreToolUse `{"tool_name":"Edit","tool_input":{"file_path":"<abs>/scratch_probe_t053.tmp"}}` into `check_lock.py` with ambient main -> stderr "HARNESS LOCK: ... write-locked by agent 'fake-cross-engine' ...", EXIT 2 (stays 2, matches T-042's pre-existing block scenario -- registration never happened so fake-cross-engine never entered session_holders()). Cleaned: `lock.py release scratch_probe_t053.tmp --holder fake-cross-engine` + rm scratch file.
(c) unregister same rule: `CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=fake-cross-engine python3 .harness/bin/session.py unregister substrate-worker-1` -> stderr REFUSED (action=unregister), EXIT 1; `session.py list` confirms substrate-worker-1 (a live holder I must not touch) is UNCHANGED/still registered. Authorized unregister exercised in (a) cleanup (EXIT 0).
(d) `--task` attribution: covered by (a) -- entry + event both carry task="T-053" (OQ2 fix confirmed).
(e) Regression: `python3 -m py_compile .harness/bin/session.py .harness/bin/harness_common.py .harness/bin/check_lock.py` -> exit 0, no output (clean). `list` output format/columns byte-identical to pre-change (NAME/REGISTERED_AT/EXPIRES_AT/BY). Expiry: `register scratch-t053-expiry --ttl 1` then sleep 2 then `list` -> entry correctly absent (pruned); `unregister scratch-t053-expiry` -> "noop: was not a live holder" EXIT 0. _coordinator_set() fail-closed sweep (monkeypatched hc.STATE in a throwaway interpreter, 5 cases): missing file/non-dict-json/malformed-registry/missing-agents-key ALL -> {'main'}; well-formed registry with an extra role=='coordinator' entry -> {'main','extra-coord'} (correctly widens only from a genuinely live, well-formed registry).

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA CHECKLIST: (1) ambient-gated register/unregister with live registry read + fail-closed fallback + refusal event + exit1 -- DONE. (2) --task flag + event field -- DONE. (3) docstring+README honest paragraph -- DONE. Probes a-e -- ALL PASS, all scratch registrations/locks cleaned up; live holders (substrate-worker-1, fable-5-coordinator, evolution-analyst, orchestration-planner) untouched; substrate-worker-2 appeared as a 5th live holder during my probe window from unrelated concurrent agent activity (registered by ambient main, not by me) -- left untouched per instructions.

DEVIATIONS/OPEN-QUESTIONS for verifier: (i) unregister has no --by flag (never had one); refusal event for unregister actions logs by=null since there's nothing to attribute -- literal spec text "by=<--by value>" only cleanly applies to register, I did not add a --by flag to unregister since the task didn't ask for one. (ii) refusal event carries an extra 'action' field (register|unregister) beyond the 3 literal fields (agent/name/by) named in the spec -- additive only, does not remove any mandated field. (iii) list_holders() output intentionally UNCHANGED (no task column) to satisfy criterion (e) literally ("list ... behavior unchanged"); task attribution is visible via events.jsonl and the raw session_holders.json entry but not via `list` -- flag if a task column in `list` was actually wanted.

HANDOFF -> verifier: Replay commands (run from repo root; scratch-named, clean up after):
1) py_compile: python3 -m py_compile .harness/bin/session.py .harness/bin/harness_common.py .harness/bin/check_lock.py  (expect exit 0, no output)
2) probe (a) T-037 replay: python3 .harness/bin/session.py register scratch-verify-a --by substrate-worker-1 --ttl 60 --task T-053  (expect exit 0, "registered:" incl task=T-053); then: python3 .harness/bin/session.py unregister scratch-verify-a  (expect exit 0)
3) probe (b) refusal: CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=fake-cross-engine python3 .harness/bin/session.py register fake-cross-engine  (expect exit 1, stderr REFUSED..., absent from `session.py list`); tail .harness/logs/events.jsonl for session_holder_register_refused agent=fake-cross-engine.
4) probe (b) full bypass repro: printf x > scratch_verify.tmp; python3 .harness/bin/lock.py acquire scratch_verify.tmp --holder fake-cross-engine --task T-053; python3 -c "import json;print(json.dumps({'tool_name':'Edit','tool_input':{'file_path':'$(pwd)/scratch_verify.tmp'}}))" | python3 .harness/bin/check_lock.py  (expect exit 2, HARNESS LOCK message); then python3 .harness/bin/lock.py release scratch_verify.tmp --holder fake-cross-engine; rm scratch_verify.tmp
5) probe (c): CLAUDE_HARNESS_AGENT_ID=fake-cross-engine python3 .harness/bin/session.py unregister substrate-worker-1  (expect exit 1, and substrate-worker-1 still present in `session.py list` afterward -- do NOT unregister real live holders)
6) probe (e) expiry regression: python3 .harness/bin/session.py register scratch-verify-e --ttl 1; sleep 2; python3 .harness/bin/session.py list | grep scratch-verify-e  (expect no match -- pruned); python3 .harness/bin/session.py unregister scratch-verify-e (expect "noop", exit 0)

Full detail (implementation summary, exact probe outputs I observed, checklist, deviations) is in the task notes above. Owned files touched: .harness/bin/session.py, .harness/README.md only (git diff --stat -- .harness/bin/session.py .harness/README.md). Live session holders (substrate-worker-1, fable-5-coordinator, evolution-analyst, orchestration-planner) verified untouched; all scratch registrations/locks I created were cleaned up.
